indication: Second or subsequent line treatment of platinum refractory ovarian cancer; Ovarian cancer where the individual is unable to tolerate platinum therapy due to, for example, allergy; WHO Performance status 0, 1, 2

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (6mg/ml). Must be administered via a non-PVC administration set containing an in-line 0.22 micron filter. Vesicant — extravasation precautions required. In patients with poor bone marrow reserves consider starting dose of 60mg/m² with a view to increase to 80mg/m² if well tolerated. This is an unlicensed dosage schedule.

pre_medications.indication: Premedication to reduce risk of hypersensitivity reaction
Premedication to reduce risk of hypersensitivity reaction

pre_medications.drug_name: H2 antagonist (e.g. ranitidine 50mg IV / famotidine 20mg oral / nizatidine 150mg oral / ranitidine 150mg oral)
pre_medications.timing: 30 minutes before chemotherapy — according to local formulary choice and availability
pre_medications.indication: Premedication to reduce risk of hypersensitivity reaction

take_home_medicines.frequency: Three times a day when required
take_home_medicines.notes: Dispensed as original pack on day 1 only

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold treatment until recovery, then decrease subsequent weekly paclitaxel dose by one dose level (level 0: 80mg/m²/wk → level -1: 70mg/m²/wk → level -2: 60mg/m²/wk)
Withhold treatment until recovery, then decrease subsequent weekly paclitaxel dose by one dose level
No dose adjustment needed for renal impairment
Decrease weekly paclitaxel dose by one dose level without interruption of therapy
Withhold therapy until resolution to NCI-CTC grade 1 or lower, then decrease dose by one level

Withhold until grade 1 or lower, then decrease subsequent weekly doses by one dose level. Stop if grade 3+ non-haematological toxicity, more than two dose reductions required, or treatment delay >2 weeks.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: For neutrophils <1x10⁹/L or platelets ≤100x10⁹/L withhold and reduce dose by one level on resumption. Consider blood transfusion if haemoglobin <8g/dL or symptomatic anaemia.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, U&Es and LFTs every 21 days starting from day 1 of cycle 1
special_warnings: Hypersensitivity reactions tend to occur with the first or second infusion. Interrupt for minor symptoms (flushing, localised rash); restart at lower rate if resolve within 5 minutes. Immediately discontinue for severe reactions (profound hypotension, bronchospasm, generalised erythema).
Paclitaxel is highly myelosuppressive; exercise caution in patients with poor bone marrow reserves (extensive prior treatment, bone metastasis, extensive skeletal radiation).
Paclitaxel is a vesicant — extravasation precautions required.
All dose reductions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval. If a third dose reduction is necessary, stop treatment.
This is an unlicensed dosage schedule.

drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (6mg/ml). Must be administered via a non-PVC administration set containing an in-line 0.22 micron filter. Vesicant — extravasation precautions required. In patients with poor bone marrow reserves consider starting dose of 60mg/m² with a view to increase to 80mg/m² if well tolerated. This is an unlicensed dosage schedule.
drugs.notes: Days 1, 8, 15 of each 21-day cycle | Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (6mg/ml). Must be administered via a non-PVC administration set containing an in-line 0.22 micron filter. Vesicant — extravasation precautions required. In patients with poor bone marrow reserves consider starting dose of 60mg/m² with a view to increase to 80mg/m² if well tolerated. This is an unlicensed dosage schedule.

indication: Rucaparib as maintenance treatment in patients with high grade epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube or primary peritoneal carcinoma who have a deleterious or suspected deleterious germline and/or somatic BRCA mutation (or without BRCA mutation) and who have a recent first or subsequent relapse of platinum-sensitive disease and who are now in response following a second or subsequent platinum-based chemotherapy. ECOG performance status 0 or 1. Full eligibility criteria per NICE/Blueteq criteria.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Tablets swallowed whole; may be taken with or without food. Doses approximately 12 hours apart. If patient vomits after taking rucaparib, do not retake — take next scheduled dose. If a dose is missed, take next dose at scheduled time. Available as 300mg, 250mg and 200mg film-coated tablets. Treatment continued until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity or patient choice to stop (12 cycles set in ARIA). Formal medical review to assess continuation at least by start of cycle 3. Oral SACT — NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed.

take_home_medicines.notes: Oral SACT — to be prescribed only under supervision of an oncologist

take_home_medicines.frequency: Three times a day when required
take_home_medicines.notes: Not included in ARIA regimen; can be added from support regimens if required

dose_modifications.notes: Dose reduction ladder: Starting dose 600mg BD → First reduction 500mg BD → Second reduction 400mg BD → Third reduction 300mg BD. If third dose reduction necessary, stop treatment.
Delay until neutrophils ≥1.5x10⁹/L with weekly FBCs. If not recovered within 4 weeks refer to haematology.
Delay until platelets ≥75x10⁹/L with weekly FBCs. If not recovered within 4 weeks refer to haematology.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 AST/ALT elevation without other signs of liver dysfunction
dose_modifications.notes: Monitor LFTs weekly until resolution to grade ≤2. Continue rucaparib provided bilirubin <ULN and alkaline phosphatase <3xULN. Interrupt if AST/ALT levels do not decline within 2 weeks until grade ≤2, then resume at same or reduced dose.
Interrupt rucaparib until values return to grade ≤2, then resume with a dose reduction and monitor LFTs weekly for 3 weeks.

dose_modifications.condition: Severe hepatic impairment (total bilirubin >3 times ULN)
dose_modifications.notes: Rucaparib not recommended in severe hepatic impairment. No starting dose adjustment for mild or moderate impairment.
No starting dose adjustment for mild or moderate renal impairment. No clinical data for CrCl <30ml/min — not recommended; use only if benefits outweigh risks.

dose_modifications.condition: Diagnosis of myelodysplastic syndrome or acute myeloid leukaemia
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue rucaparib if MDS or AML diagnosed.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Eligible levels to initiate a new cycle: neutrophils ≥1.5x10⁹/L and platelets ≥75x10⁹/L. FBC, U&Es, creatinine, LFTs every 28 days for first 4 cycles, then may switch to every 56 days.

monitoring_requirements: BRCA testing prior to starting therapy
FBC, U&Es, creatinine, LFTs every 28 days for the first four cycles (prior to day 1 of each 28-day cycle)
After cycle 4: FBC, U&Es, creatinine and LFTs may be switched to every 56 days
Weekly FBCs if treatment delayed for haematological toxicity
special_warnings: Photosensitivity: patients should avoid direct sunlight, wear protective clothing, and use sunscreen/lip balm with SPF 50 or greater when outside.
0.5% of patients in clinical trials developed MDS or AML — monitor for signs of weakness, fatigue, fever, weight loss, infections, bleeding, bruising, breathlessness and haematological toxicities.
Rucaparib may have a minor influence on ability to drive and use machines — caution in patients reporting fatigue, nausea or dizziness.
Rucaparib interacts with other agents — always check for drug interactions.
Must only be prescribed under supervision of an oncologist.
NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert must be followed.
All dose reductions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval.

required_patient_fields.brca_mutation_status: germline and/or somatic BRCA testing required prior to starting
required_patient_fields.prior_platinum_response: partial or complete response to second or subsequent line platinum-based chemotherapy

drugs.special_instructions: Tablets swallowed whole; may be taken with or without food. Doses approximately 12 hours apart. Treatment continued until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity or patient choice to stop (12 cycles set in ARIA). Formal medical review to assess continuation at least by start of cycle 3. Oral SACT — NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed.

indication: Second-line or subsequent treatment of platinum-refractory or platinum-resistant ovarian cancer, or in women who are allergic to platinum-based compounds, for whom pegylated liposomal doxorubicin and single-agent paclitaxel are considered inappropriate. WHO performance status 0, 1, 2. Palliative intent.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Daily on days 1-5 of each 21-day cycle
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Dose rounded to nearest 0.4mg (up if halfway). Topotecan is an exfoliant — extravasation precautions required.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Three times a day when required for nausea

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery to 1.5x10⁹/L or greater, then continue with reduced dose of 1.25mg/m²
Delay until recovery to 100x10⁹/L or greater, then continue with reduced dose of 1.25mg/m² if the platelet count fell below 25x10⁹/L or symptomatic bleeding occurred

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or above non-haematological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below, then reduce dose to 3mg/m² or discontinue as appropriate

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Prior to cycle 1: neutrophils ≥1.5x10⁹/L and platelets ≥100x10⁹/L. Haemoglobin should reach 9g/dL prior to each cycle — support with blood transfusions if necessary.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day 1 of each cycle
CA125 prior to day 1 of each cycle
special_warnings: Topotecan is an exfoliant — extravasation precautions required.
All dose reductions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval. If a third dose reduction is necessary, stop treatment.
Support with blood transfusions if haemoglobin below 9g/dL prior to each cycle.

required_patient_fields.renal_function: Creatinine clearance required prior to treatment
required_patient_fields.prior_treatment: Second-line or subsequent; pegylated liposomal doxorubicin and single-agent paclitaxel must be considered inappropriate

indication: Recurrent platinum sensitive ovarian, peritoneal or fallopian tube cancer; WHO performance status 0, 1, 2

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded according to CSCCN agreed bands. First infusion over 90 minutes; if well tolerated second infusion may be given over 60 minutes; if well tolerated subsequent infusions may be given over 30 minutes. Bevacizumab — neutral (extravasation). Treatment continued until unacceptable toxicity or disease progression (six cycles set in ARIA). DOSE REDUCTIONS ARE NOT PERMITTED — doses should be omitted (not reduced) for adverse reactions. If more than two doses are missed due to adverse events, stop treatment. Half-life approximately 20 days.

dose_modifications.notes: Little need to adjust dose of bevacizumab for haematological toxicity. Consider blood transfusion if haemoglobin <8g/dL or symptomatic anaemia.
No information available on hepatic dose adjustment.
No information available on renal dose adjustment.
Doses should be OMITTED and NOT REDUCED. If more than two doses missed, stop treatment.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or above haemorrhagic event (requiring blood transfusion or major non-elective intervention)
NCI-CTC grade 3 or above congestive heart failure or left ventricular dysfunction
NCI-CTC symptomatic grade 4 venous thromboembolic event
dose_modifications.notes: Stop bevacizumab. Exception: if pulmonary embolism, may restart once full recovery made and patient anti-coagulated with subcutaneous LMWH (oral anticoagulant must NOT be used).

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or above hypertension that is persistent
NCI-CTC grade 1 or first occurrence of grade 2 proteinuria
Second occurrence of NCI-CTC grade 2 or any grade 3 proteinuria
dose_modifications.notes: Conduct 24-hour urine collection or UPCR within 3 days before next dose. If <2g protein/24h or UPCR 0-1: administer bevacizumab and return to dipstick monitoring. If >2g protein/24h: omit bevacizumab, repeat 24-hour urine before next scheduled dose.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Blood pressure and dipstick urinalysis for proteinuria required prior to each treatment with bevacizumab. FBC, LFTs, U&Es, EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance, and CA125 prior to each cycle.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle
EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance prior to each cycle
Blood pressure and dipstick urinalysis for proteinuria prior to each treatment with bevacizumab
special_warnings: DOSE REDUCTIONS ARE NOT PERMITTED for bevacizumab — doses must be OMITTED (not reduced) for adverse reactions. If more than two doses are missed due to adverse events, stop treatment.
Haemorrhage risk: monitor for haemorrhagic events. Stop for NCI-CTC grade ≥3 haemorrhage.
Hypertension is a common consequence of bevacizumab therapy — monitor blood pressure at each visit.
Gastrointestinal perforation: stop permanently if this occurs.
Impaired wound healing: bevacizumab should not be initiated for at least 28 days following major surgery.
Arterial thromboembolism: stop permanently.
Venous thromboembolism: stop for symptomatic grade 4 VTE; may restart for PE if anticoagulated with LMWH (not oral anticoagulant).
Proteinuria: monitor with dipstick urinalysis; if grade 2+ at second occurrence or grade 3, perform 24-hour urine collection or UPCR.
Half-life of bevacizumab is approximately 20 days — discontinuation not expected to influence short-term clinical evolution of adverse events.
This protocol may require funding approval.
All dose reductions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval.

required_patient_fields.renal_function: EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance required
required_patient_fields.blood_pressure: Required at each visit prior to treatment
required_patient_fields.urinalysis: Dipstick urinalysis for proteinuria required prior to each treatment
